findings.evidence: Line 55 has `[CommGroup G] [CommGroup H]`; lines 62-66 define the equivalence only by composing `MonoidAlgebra.prodTensorBialgEquiv R` with `AffineGroup.Product.pointsMulEquiv`, while `MonoidAlgebra.prodTensorBialgEquiv` is already proved for `[Monoid G] [Monoid H]` in `TauCeti/Algebra/Bialgebra/MonoidAlgebraProduct.lean:47,145` and Mathlib gives `MonoidAlgebra R M` a commutative semiring from `[CommMonoid M]` (`mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/MonoidAlgebra/Defs.lean:723`).
findings.fix: Prove a general version under `[CommMonoid G] [CommMonoid H]` (probably outside or before the diagonalizable-group specialization) and derive/alias the current group case from it if the diagonalizable namespace wants group wording.
findings.issue: `prodPointsMulEquiv` and its three simp lemmas require `[CommGroup G] [CommGroup H]`, but their statements/proofs only use the commutative monoid algebra bialgebra and the units `1 : G`, `1 : H`; no inverse operation or Hopf structure appears.

summary: The bialgebra product API is at the right monoid level, but the points equivalence is stated only for groups even though the construction is naturally commutative-monoid level.
